brief_document.title: Workflow step runner fix and integration verification
brief_document.purpose: Fix the make_norn_step_runner session persistence gap (events lost when workflow steps complete) and verify the complete integration: WebSocket compatibility, existing test preservation, event translation pipeline tests, namespace tool tests, session persistence tests, and line count compliance.
brief_document.task: Wire .session(store) in make_norn_step_runner with workflow-scoped EventStore at {data_dir}/sessions/workflow/{step_id}.jsonl. Verify that the WebSocket handler, session replay, and resubscribe mechanism work unchanged with Norn sessions. Add integration tests covering the full event translation pipeline, namespace tool actions, and session persistence flows. Verify no file exceeds 500 lines of code and clippy passes clean.

requirements.title: Fix make_norn_step_runner session persistence
requirements.spec: WHEN make_norn_step_runner builds an AgentBuilder for a workflow step, THE SYSTEM SHALL call .session(store) with a JsonlSink-backed EventStore. The session file SHALL be stored at {data_dir}/sessions/workflow/{execution_id}_{step_number}.jsonl. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use bare EventStore::new() for workflow steps.
acceptance: AgentBuilder::session(store) is called with a JsonlSink-backed EventStore
Session file path uses execution_id and step_number
Workflow session files are at {data_dir}/sessions/workflow/
Events persist to disk during workflow step execution

requirements.title: WebSocket compatibility verification
requirements.spec: The system SHALL verify that ws/assistant.rs, ws/session_replay.rs, and try_resubscribe work unchanged with Norn-powered sessions. No modifications to these files. Verification is by inspection and integration test.
acceptance: ws/assistant.rs has no modifications from this cluster
ws/session_replay.rs has no modifications from this cluster
WS handler subscribes to broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent> from a Norn session
try_resubscribe works when Norn broadcast sender drops and the session re-wakes

requirements.title: Clippy and existing test preservation
requirements.spec: cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings SHALL pass clean after all briefs land. All existing assistant service tests SHALL pass without modification.
acceptance: cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0
All tests in service.rs, session_loop.rs, translate.rs, events.rs pass unchanged
No existing test is modified or deleted

requirements.title: Norn session loop integration tests
requirements.spec: The system SHALL include tests verifying the complete event translation pipeline: a mock Norn agent emitting ProviderEvents → NornTranslateState producing AssistantEvents → broadcast channel delivery. Tests SHALL verify TextDelta, ToolUseStarted, ToolResult, TurnComplete, and SubAgentEvent lifecycle.
acceptance: Tests in norn_translate.rs cover all ProviderEvent → AssistantEvent mappings
Tests verify SessionStarted synthesis before first event
Tests verify sub-agent wrapping for child agent_ids
Tests verify turn boundary state clearing

requirements.title: Namespace tool tests
requirements.spec: Each namespace tool SHALL have tests verifying all commands with mocked service layer. Tests SHALL verify input_schema correctness, command dispatch, authorization scoping to CallerContext.member_id, and structured JSON output.
acceptance: MessagingTool tests cover all 12 commands
MemberTool tests cover all 7 commands
WorkspaceTool tests cover all 4 commands
Tests use mock ServiceContext
Tests verify CallerContext authorization scoping

requirements.title: Session persistence tests
requirements.spec: The system SHALL include tests verifying NornSessionStore create, resume, and fork flows. Tests SHALL verify JSONL file creation, event replay on resume, file copy on fork, and missing file fallback.
acceptance: Test: new session creates JSONL file at expected path
Test: resume reads existing JSONL and pre-populates EventStore
Test: fork copies source file and builds from copy
Test: missing file on resume falls back to new session
Tests use tempdir for file operations

requirements.title: Line count compliance
requirements.spec: No file created or modified by this cluster SHALL exceed 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, and whitespace). WHEN a file approaches the limit, THE SYSTEM SHALL split it into focused sub-modules.
acceptance: tokei reports ≤500 code lines for every file in the cluster
norn_session.rs, norn_translate.rs, and each namespace tool file are under 500 lines

brief_document.dependency_setup: NR-008 test requirements for meridian-tools dev-deps (established by NR-006): tempfile.workspace=true, tokio={workspace=true, features=["macros","rt-multi-thread","test-util"]}. No other new dependencies needed — meridian-services already has all required dev-deps.
boundaries: SHALL NOT modify ws/assistant.rs or ws/session_replay.rs
SHALL NOT modify existing tests — only add new ones
SHALL NOT modify the norn crate
SHALL NOT exceed 500 lines of code per file
verification: cargo test --workspace passes all existing and new tests
tokei confirms all files under 500 code lines
WebSocket handler works with Norn sessions (integration test or manual verification)

checklist.text: make_norn_step_runner calls .session() on AgentBuilder with workflow-scoped EventStore
ws/assistant.rs compiles and passes all existing tests without modification after all NR-* briefs land
ws/session_replay compiles and passes all existing tests without modification after all NR-* briefs land
WS resubscribe (try_resubscribe) handles Norn broadcast sender drop identically to Claude Runner drop
All existing assistant service tests pass without modification
Norn session loop tests verify complete event translation pipeline
Namespace tool tests verify all actions with mocked service layer
Session persistence tests verify create, resume, and fork flows
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)

stories.text: As a developer, I want to see which runtime a session is using so that I can diagnose runtime-specific issues.
As a developer, I want the activity panel to show identical content for Norn and Claude Runner sessions so that I can verify event parity.
As the system, I want Norn sessions to persist to both JSONL and PG so that session resume and UI history are independently served.
As the system, I want workflow step sessions to persist their Norn EventStore to disk so that step conversation history is not lost when the step completes.

constraints.text: No new external dependencies. Namespace tools call service-layer functions via ServiceContext, not HTTP.
AssistantEvent is the single event type for all downstream consumers. Both runtimes produce it.
JSONL session files are Norn's source of truth for resume. PG is the UI's source of truth. Neither replaces the other.
The model sees at most 25 tools (16 standard Norn + up to 7 namespace + 2 optional web). Profile filtering reduces this in practice.
Session IDs are UUIDs shared between the JSONL filename, PG assistant_session_events key, and SessionHandle registry key.
Wake prompt delivery mechanism changes, format does not.
mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and re-exports.
No file over 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace).
All errors handled via thiserror in library code. No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code.
Namespace tools implement the existing Tool trait. No changes to the trait interface.

resolved_context.intention: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude Runner stays only for legacy background task dispatch until it can be retired entirely. Member-facing sessions run as typed, observable, in-process agent loops rather than opaque subprocess invocations. Session state persists across visits without relying on a CLI binary's internal persistence. The model sees a focused set of domain tools instead of a raw shell with 175 CLI commands. This is the June 15 deadline work.

provenance.quote: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude R
